evidence_quotes.quote: Ban dark patterns and manipulation techniques: designs that divert attention or exploit cognitive biases undermine freedom of choice and must be prohibited.

Limiting profiling and personalised pricing that exploit vulnerabilities: algorithms should not be allowed to adjust prices or offers based on sensitive data (age, financial situation, state of health, etc.) without informed consent.
I also argue that social media algorithms should be redesigned to diversify the content presented, so that users are not locked into public opinion bubbles and open up.

Regulating the deceptive practices of influencers: failure to disclose or promote harmful products must be sanctioned, so that hearings can clearly distinguish advertising content from editorial content.

Eliminating unfair terms in digital contracts: automatic renewals, free testing that becomes paid subscriptions without clear information, or automated dialogues that prevent termination should be prohibited.
Prohibit forms of drippricing, misleading prices and discounts that mislead: price transparency is essential for consumers to be able to effectively compare offers.

paragraphs.text: I am in favour of strong measures that protect consumers, especially the most vulnerable, such as: Ban dark patterns and manipulation techniques: designs that divert attention or exploit cognitive biases undermine freedom of choice and must be prohibited. Remove addictive design mechanisms, especially when targeting minors: games, applications or services that encourage excessive or even compulsive use harm the mental and physical health of young people. Limiting profiling and personalised pricing that exploit vulnerabilities: algorithms should not be allowed to adjust prices or offers based on sensitive data (age, financial situation, state of health, etc.) without informed consent. Regulating the deceptive practices of influencers: failure to disclose or promote harmful products must be sanctioned, so that hearings can clearly distinguish advertising content from editorial content. Prohibit forms of drippricing, misleading prices and discounts that mislead: price transparency is essential for consumers to be able to effectively compare offers. Eliminating unfair terms in digital contracts: automatic renewals, free testing that becomes paid subscriptions without clear information, or automated dialogues that prevent termination should be prohibited. I also argue that social media algorithms should be redesigned to diversify the content presented, so that users are not locked into public opinion bubbles and open up. With regard to young people, I think it is legitimate to introduce reliable age checks and, in some cases, a digital cover which limits access to platforms at night. Finally, I am convinced that: Advertisements that encourage the purchase of goods that are unnecessary or harmful to the environment must be prohibited. Hosting and content distribution platforms must have a clear responsibility: they must ensure that advertisements are legitimate, comply with safety standards and respect the environment. Advertising providers should be held liable for fraudulent or deceptive campaigns. Severe sanctions are needed when promoting scams or non-compliant products, in order to protect consumers and restore confidence in the advertising market. From experience, I have seen a lot of misleading advertising on large audience sites. It should be possible to issue an alert to the authorities. These measures aim to create a fairer, safer and more respectful framework for users’ fundamental rights, while encouraging responsible business practices.
